title: 5270R, Perpetual Calendar Chronograph, Pink Gold
description: A ref. 5270/1R-001, the only 5270 reference made with a matching bracelet. Debuting in 2018, this pink gold 5270R revived bracelets within the Perpetual Calendar Chronograph, with a black dial for the occasion. The 5270 was Patek Philippe's first entirely in-house designed, manufactured, and finished Perpetual Calendar Chronograph. Since Patek Philippe invented the Perpetual Calendar Chronograph i

title: 3970EJ, Second Series, Perpetual Calendar Chronograph, Yellow Gold
description: A Second Series 3970 with English calendar in yellow gold, the original case metal offering for the 3970. The 3970 succeeded the 2499 in 1986, the most significant contribution of Philippe Stern’s tenure over the brand. It features a 36mm Ateliers Réunis case, Lemania ébauche calibre CH 27-70 Q, feuille hands of Early Series, ‘American Typewriter’ font, and warmer Argenté dial of Early Series. It

title: Mirage 38, Prussian Blue, White Gold
description: A Mirage 38, the first watch from independent Berneron. The Mirage is the first wristwatch which draws its shape from its calibre, rather than simply being designed then given a standard ébauche. Its 2.33mm thin calibre is made of solid gold, as are the dial, case, and even springbars. The design stems from an oversized mainspring, which the case wraps around. All typography is Berneron's own care

title: Tank Cintrée, 100th Anniversary, Yellow Gold
description: A Tank Cintrée made in 2021 for the 100th Anniversary of the Cintrée case, a limited edition of 150 examples. Its dial is a mirror of the original 1921, serif for serif, with a simple Cartier-only signature. In addition, the handset references the earliest years of Cartier with Breguet or Pomme style in blued steel. The 100th Anniversary features a distinctly thinner case (compared to the contempo

title: 2577R, Calatrava, Enamel Dial, Pink Gold
description: A ref. 2577 in pink gold, 1 of 4 examples known and the best-preserved by some margin. The 2577 is an elusive and esoteric reference, with just twelve examples known in total. In addition to the 2526 and its successor the 3428, it is the third Calatrava to feature a grand feu enamel dial. With two thick layers and a silver base, the tone of this dial is a warm cream. title: 217.64.40, Reverso Tourbillon, Platinum
description: The Reverso Tourbillon Number Two was limited to only 500 examples, just the second Reverso Tourbillon model from Jaeger-LeCoultre. Its 18k white gold calibre 848 brought finer materials and finishing while maintaining design references to the brand's first pocket watch tourbillon, the calibre 170. It seamlessly blends JLC's most enduring model with its heritage in high complication movement manuf

title: 6350, Explorer, Honeycomb Dial, Steel
description: A ref. 6350 Explorer featuring pencil hands, a lollipop seconds, and incredible honeycomb Swiss-only dial. The 6350 was the first model to feature the foundational elements of the Explorer as we known them across all examples: Explorer text, 3-6-9 configuration, and chronometer certification all on the dial. In addition, it is the only Explorer reference which offers the charm of the honeycomb dia

title: 3970EP, 'Big Hallmark' Second Series, Perpetual Calendar Chronograph, Platinum
description: A Second Series 3970 in platinum, the singular known example combining an Italian calendar with the desirable Big Hallmark. Just 11 Second Series examples were produced in platinum, the rarest standard production metal, only two in Italian. The rarity of this configuration is matched by that of its condition, its case and dial unrestored and exceptional. The 3970 succeeded the 2499 in 1986, the mo

title: 5402ST, Royal Oak, C-Series, Steel
description: A ref. 5402ST C-Series Royal Oak, from the very first years of the Royal Oak. This is the origin of the classic integrated bracelet sports watch, now more revered than ever. Designed by Gérald Genta and unveiled at Basel in 1972, it was a turning point for watch design, case-making, and dial-making with its petite tapisserie. Featuring a 7mm thin case and integrated bracelet, it began a category.

title: 1941 Grönograaf, Steel
description: A Grönograaf, the very first chronograph from brothers Bart and Tim Grönefeld. Advancing the chronograph complication with bespoke architecture, it features a landmark 'soft reset' system through use of a centrifugal governor. title: Chonomètre Souverain, Black Label, Platinum
description: A Black Label Chonomètre Souverain, amongst the rarest time-only FP Journe made. Separated by black lacquer dials in platinum cases, Black Label FP Journe are amongst the brand's most exclusive productions, designed to reward its most loyal clients. This is an early production example featuring guilloché dial, no petite seconds numerals, and printed hour numerals. One of the most desirable Chronom

title: Tank Louis, NSO, Platinum
description: A Tank Louis NSO with salmon dial, platinum case, and ruby cabochon. The Cartier Tank has become immediately recognizable even beyond the watch collecting community. Blued steel Breguet hands provide lovely contrast with the true-salmon tone, a subtle nod to those found on some early pre-1940s examples before sword hands became standard. Though this custom order Tank Louis was designed through Car

title: 25654ST, Royal Oak Perpetual Calendar, Salmon Dial, Steel
description: A ref. 25654ST with a special-request salmon dial, one of just a handful made. This is one of 315 examples of the 25654 in steel. The 25654 was Audemars Piguet's effort at blending high watchmaking complication with the utilitarian and disruptive Royal Oak case. Its production, alongside the ref. 5554, comprises the core of pre-leap year Royal Oak Perpetual Calendar collecting. The 25654 embodies

title: Élégante 48, Titalyt
description: An Élégante Titalyt 48, featuring a 7.95mm thin ‘Tortue’ grade 5 titanium case with Titalyt treatment and one of the most innovative quartz calibres ever devised. FP Journe’s calibre 1210 took eight years of development and includes a sleep function, where after 35 minutes of motionlessness, the hands will stop. The opening on the dial is for a small gold oscillating mass, a mechanical element whi

title: 2526R, Calatrava, Freccero Signature, Pink Gold
description: A ref. 2526R Calatrava with double signed Freccero first series enamel dial. During production of the 2526 in the 1950s enamel dials were standard practice but Patek now reserves the process only for special editions. The Freccero signature, a prominent Uruguayan retailer, adds additional context to this example’s personal history. It features Patek’s first ever automatic calibre, 12-600 AT, and p

title: Insight Micro-Rotor, Natural Titanium
description: A Insight Micro-Rotor, 1 of 10 examples made in natural titanium, specified here with a white grand feu enamel dial. The first self-winding Romain Gauthier, the Insight MR features a 22k bi-directional winding mass supported by two pivots (top and bottom), dual mainspring barrels, dial-side balance, and contemporary yet manual finishing techniques. Conceived entirely at Romain Gauthier in the Vall

title: Chronomètre Bleu, Tantalum
description: A Chronomètre Bleu, François-Paul’s most restrained three-hand model. The Chronomètre Bleu features a 39mm tantalum case, blue dial crafted from multiple layers of hand-polished lacquer, and calibre 1304 with dual mainspring barrels and floating balance. The Chronomètre Bleu is one of very few independent watchmaker's efforts which can truly be said to have become a modern classic already.

title: Quadruple Tourbillon Secret, Pink Gold
description: A Quadruple Tourbillon Secret, 1 of 8 examples made. The Quadruple Tourbillon is a thoroughbred meant to pursue mechanical precision alone. title: Tank Cintrée, NSO, Platinum
description: A Tank Cintrée NSO produced through Cartier’s New Special Order program for one of their top clients. The project spanned 3 years resulting in a bespoke configuration; it features a platinum case, luminous Roman numerals, luminous Breguet hands, black gloss dial, and matching bloodstone cabochon. While this particular Cintrée was the product of a custom project, its design is incredibly intentiona

title: 5004J, Perpetual Calendar Split Seconds Chronograph, Yellow Gold
description: A ref. 5004J, the first production Perpetual Calendar Split Seconds Chronograph, an ultimate conclusion to the development of complicated Lemania architecture. Out of an estimated 1000 examples made across all metals, roughly 300 are thought to be in yellow gold. Reportedly hailed by Philippe Stern as the 'spiritual successor to the 2499', the 5004 is easily amongst the most important and desirabl

title: 5512, Submariner, Explorer Dial, Steel
description: An Explorer dial ref. 5512 Submariner from 1961. Within early crown guard Submariners, this 5512 represents the rapid iteration Rolex sustained at the start of the Submariner. Featuring a coveted gilt, exclamation 3-6-9 Explorer dial (Variant I), this is amongst the most desirable Submariners made. This is the last reference to combine the thorough legibility of a 'Explorer' dial with the Submarin

title: Fifty Fathoms 'Barakuda', Steel
description: A Fifty Fathoms 'Barakuda' featuring tritium and red two-tone indices for improved legibility. Born of the collaboration between Blancpain and the French Navy with the aim of providing a more functional diving tool, the Fifty Fathoms featured numerous novel innovations now common to the category. title: 116.032, Lange 1 Time Zone, Pink Gold
description: A ref. 116.032 Lange 1 Time Zone in pink gold, the third complication to be offered within the Lange 1 collection. The Lange 1’s asymmetric but geometrically defined dial design is in itself an embodiment of the teutonic principles that have guided the brand since its rebirth in 1994, here augmented to include an button-advancing second time zone display. title: 6238, 'Pre-Daytona' Chronograph, Yellow Gold
description: A ref. 6238 Pre-Daytona in scarcely seek 18 karat yellow gold. This was last Rolex to simply say 'Rolex Chronograph' on the dial at 12. In addition, the dial is monochromatic, with a sterile steel bezel and tachymeter on the dial not bezel. All of these austere charms changed in the following ref. 6239 Daytona. title: 5990/1A, Nautilus Travel Time Chronograph, Tiffany Dial, Steel
description: A ref. 5990/1A-001 Nautilus Travel Time Chronograph. Debuting in 2014, the 5990 incorporates two highly desired complications in one ultimate sporting package. This is one of just a handful of 5990A examples observed with a Tiffany signature subtly within the 6 subdial, reserved for the very best clients of the brand, on the discontinued grey dial: creating something of an apex 5990A.

title: 2915-1, Omega Speedmaster, Steel
description: A ref. 2915-1 Speedmaster, the first series of the first Speedmaster reference. A watch purpose built for racing drivers, its design focussed on precision and legibility. Its dial is high contrast, featuring the now famous broad arrow hand set. The 38.5mm straight lug case houses the most technically advanced movement used in a tool watch at the time, the calibre 321. title: 25720BA, Star Wheel, Yellow Gold
description: A ref. 25720BA Star Wheel with a guilloché dial. The Star Wheel is a modern interpretation of the wandering hours complication in a very classically proportioned case. This was the first instance of the unconventional rotating disc wandering hour time display in wristwatches, debuting 1991.
